title: 3 Die After Suspected Exposure to Unknown Substance in New Mexico
description: More than a dozen emergency officials were hospitalized after responding to a home in a town near Albuquerque. The authorities said there was no threat to the public.
author: Yan Zhuang

title: Colorado Governor Censured for Commuting Sentence of Election Denier
description: The Colorado State Democratic Party, reflecting the anger of rank-and-file Democratic voters, rebuked Gov. Jared Polis on Wednesday over his decision to release Tina Peters from prison.
author: Jack Healy

title: A $5 Million Donation From Big Tobacco Preceded F.D.A. Vape Decision
description: Reynolds American’s contribution to MAGA Inc. came about one week before the Trump administration announced a new policy on e-cigarettes the company had sought.
author: Kenneth P. Vogel and Christina Jewett

title: Despite Problems, Boeing Sought Less Inspection of Part Involved in UPS Crash
description: The National Transportation Board has been investigating the cause behind a plane crash in Louisville, Ky., that killed 15 people last year.
author: Billy Witz

title: He Was Jailed Over a Charlie Kirk Post. The Sheriff Now Owes Him $835,000.
description: Larry Bushart sued a Tennessee sheriff who claimed he wanted to incite hysteria with a post after Mr. Kirk’s killing and jailed him for 37 days.
author: Rick Rojas

title: Trump’s Praise of Spencer Pratt Could Doom Him in L.A. Mayor’s Race
description: Democrats have been trying to portray Mr. Pratt, a former reality TV star, as a MAGA Republican in the mayor’s race. President Trump just gave them a helping hand.
author: Soumya Karlamangla

title: G.O.P. May Bear The Cost of Trump’s Unpopularity
description: Donald Trump’s endorsed candidates are winning Republican primaries across the country, but the president’s unpopularity with the broader electorate could drag the party down in the general election, the Times political correspondent Shane Goldmacher explains.
author: Shane Goldmacher, Nour Idriss, Stephanie Swart and Rafaela Balster

title: Nimitz Aircraft Carrier Enters Caribbean as Trump Pressures Cuba
description: The carrier arrived in the southern Caribbean on the same day that the Justice Department announced charges against Raul Castro.
author: Eric Schmitt

title: Justice Dept. Charges Raúl Castro as Trump Escalates Pressure Campaign Against Cuba
description: The indictment was an extraordinary escalation of the Trump administration’s multifaceted pressure campaign against Cuba’s Communist government.
author: Alan Feuer, Frances Robles and David C. Adams

title: Thomas Massie, a Trump Critic, Causes Latest Midterm Fight Over Israel
description: In primary races across the country, debates over Israel are taking center stage, dividing Democrats and Republicans alike.
author: Lisa Lerer and Jennifer Medina

title: Trump’s Government Moves to Spare an Unhappy Taxpayer Named Trump
description: No president has ever used the federal government to advance his own personal interests and those of his family and allies as expansively and openly as Mr. Trump has.
author: Peter Baker

title: Judge Rules That White House Officials Must Follow Presidential Records Act
description: The judge overruled a government memo from April, which rejected a records law that mandates preservation of all official presidential documents.
author: Minho Kim

title: Faulty Part in UPS Plane Crash Was Often Overlooked, Witnesses Say
description: Investigators suggested that infrequent maintenance requirements, among other issues, appeared to contribute to the faulty part that caused the crash, which killed 15 people last year.
author: Billy Witz and Karoun Demirjian

title: Trump’s Coast Guard Address: ‘Good Looking Men’ and Deja Vu
description: President Trump’s commencement address was a mixture of jokes and praise for the seafaring life of the Coast Guard graduates. But there were detours into choppy waters.
author: Shawn McCreesh

title: Senate G.O.P. Ready to Drop Ballroom Funds From ICE Bill
description: Several Republicans had balked at including the money in an immigration enforcement measure that the party plans to push through on a party-line vote.
author: Michael Gold

title: How the $1.8 Billion Trump Fund May Violate Past Practice and Policy
description: The fund that could offer payouts to Trump allies who accuse the government of mistreatment is not only highly unusual but also appears to violate the administration’s own policies.
author: Devlin Barrett

title: Trump Gets His Payback in Primary Elections, but It Comes at a Cost in Congress
description: Republican senators are angry the president is working to unseat their colleagues. But he is also creating more free agents in his own party in Congress willing to defy him.
author: Carl Hulse

title: Robert Woodson, Leader of the Black Conservative Movement, Dies at 89
description: A veteran community activist, he believed self-reliance — rather than affirmative action or government help — was the way to fight racism, poverty and crime.
author: Robert D. McFadden

title: Prosecutor Is Charged With Stealing Sealed Report on Trump Documents Case
description: Prosecutors accused Carmen Lineberger of emailing records related to Jack Smith’s investigation to a personal account, charging her with a felony punishable with 20 years in prison.
author: Chris Cameron

title: A Founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics Gave Up the High Life to Be a Catholic Priest
description: A mystical encounter 12 years ago led Scott Vincent Borba, a founder of E.L.F. Beauty, on a journey that will culminate this week, when he is ordained as a Roman Catholic priest.
author: Michael Levenson
